data.title: LMT Stock Sees Biggest Single-Day Rally In Nearly 25 Years As Lockheed Posts Record Backlog – Retail Says Defense Sector Getting Harder To Ignore
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lockheed Martin raised its fiscal 2026 sales guidance to $79.75 billion to $81.75 billion, up from its previous forecast of $77.50 billion to $80.00 billion.
  • The company’s second quarter sales came in at $20.1 billion, beating Street estimates of $19.3 billion, according to Fiscal.ai data.
  • LMT breached its 100-day moving average for the first time in over three months.

Defense giant Lockheed Martin (LMT) was in the limelight on Thursday, with shares posting their biggest single-day gain since September 2001, after the company delivered stronger-than-expected second-quarter results, raised its full-year outlook, and reported a record backlog.

At the time of writing, LMT shares were up 11% and breached their 100-day moving average (100-DMA) for the first time in over three months.

LMT Ups Fiscal 2026 Guidance As Backlogs Hit All-Time High

Lockheed Martin raised its fiscal 2026 sales guidance to $79.75 billion to $81.75 billion, up from its previous forecast of $77.50 billion to $80.00 billion, while lifting its earnings per share outlook to $29.95 to $30.65 from $29.35 to $30.25.

The Maryland-headquartered firm said its long-term growth outlook is supported by several major contract wins, including a $35 billion THAAD contract, a $3 billion GMLRS award, a $1.1 billion HIMARS deal, and a $2.3 billion radar contract, which are expected to drive production and expand its backlog, which came in at a record $230 billion in Q2.

Management also cited sustained demand for the F-35 fighter jet, increasing orders from allied nations, and a growing munitions backlog as key drivers for future growth.

“Our backlog reached a new all-time high, now $230 billion. We generated nearly $3 billion in free cash flow. We accelerated our revenue growth and turned in higher earnings per share. Consequently, we are raising our 2026 guidance, reflecting our confidence that our strategy is gaining momentum, and we will continue to produce sustained profitable growth over the next few years,” said CEO Jim Taiclet in a call with analysts.

Q2 Sales Boosted By Gains Across All Segments

Lockheed Martin reported a strong second quarter, with sales rising 11% year over year to $20.1 billion, beating Wall Street estimates of $19.3 billion, according to Fiscal.ai data. The company said the increase was driven by growth across aeronautics, missiles and fire control, rotary and mission systems, and space segments.

Net earnings increased by $1.5 billion, while earnings per share (EPS) rose to $7.94, above Street estimates of $7.18. The improvement was primarily driven by a sharp increase in operating profit, benefiting from the absence of program-related losses recorded a year earlier, along with favorable pension-related adjustments, the company said.

Retail Says Defense Sector Momentum To Continue

Retail sentiment surrounding LMT on Stocktwits changed to ‘extremely bullish’ from ‘bullish’ over the past 24 hours, amid a 270% increase in message volumes.

One user said “a stronger backlog means better future revenue visibility,” adding that the defense sector is “getting harder for investors to ignore”.

View this Stocktwits post

Another user said the “defence cycle doesn’t look like a one-quarter event.”

View this Stocktwits post

LMT shares have gained around 15% so far this year.

Also read: BATL, CVX, XOM Stocks Rise As Brent Crude Tops $100 – Trump Threatens Iran With 'Major Military Punishment' After Houthi Attack On Saudi Ships

MTL: LTL margins topped 20% as margin focus and project readiness drove a standout quarter
data.description:
Margin improvement and operational execution drove one of the best quarters ever, with LTL margins exceeding 20% and strong positioning for major capital projects. CapEx was increased to secure equipment for anticipated nation-building projects, and guidance suggests potential upside if trends persist.

data.description:

Result of AGM

The voting results from the AGM will be made available on the Company's website, www.orchardfundinggroupplc.com.

Elektros Announces Strategic Positioning to Address AI-Driven Energy Cost Surge and Grid Constraints
data.description:
WEST PALM BEACH, FL / ACCESS Newswire / July 23, 2026 / ELEKTROS Inc. (OTC PINK:ELEK), an emerging company focused on energy solutions, today issued a statement addressing the accelerating U.S. energy cost surge tied to the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure, positioning the Company at the center of emerging solutions to stabilize power markets.

Recent Federal Reserve commentary and U.S. Energy Information Administration data confirm that AI-driven data center demand is creating a structural supply shock across national power grids. Electricity consumption is now reaching record levels, with commercial demand surpassing residential usage for the first time. In key regions such as the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast, grid capacity constraints have already triggered sharp increases in wholesale energy pricing, translating into residential utility hikes of up to 14% in high-density data center corridors.

This surge is not isolated to utilities. Rising industrial power costs are cascading through the broader economy, increasing expenses for logistics, food production, healthcare systems, and manufacturing. As a result, energy is emerging as a primary driver of sustained inflationary pressure into the 2026-2027 cycle.

Elektros is actively developing and evaluating infrastructure strategies designed to mitigate these pressures. The Company's focus includes localized power generation, energy optimization technologies, and scalable frameworks intended to support high-demand AI environments without transferring disproportionate costs to consumers.

Management believes the current environment represents a structural shift in energy economics, where efficient power access becomes a critical competitive advantage. Elektros is positioning itself to address this imbalance by aligning energy innovation with the accelerating demands of AI infrastructure, aiming to deliver long-term value for both shareholders and the broader energy ecosystem.

The full "Data Center Energy Solutions" research report is available for immediate download on the company's website: https://elektros.energy/reports/data-center-energy-research.pdf

Join our energy technology newsletter to receive timely updates on corporate milestones, software deployment metrics, and clean energy expansion plans: https://www.elektros.energy/investors

About Elektros, Inc. Elektros, Inc. (OTC PINK:ELEK) is focused on the development of artisanal hard-rock lithium mining operations in Sierra Leone, West Africa. The Company's strategy centers on lithium exploration, development, and export to U.S.-based refining partners.

More information: www.elektros.energy

Forward-Looking Statements This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements involve risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied, including regulatory developments, execution risks, market conditions, and technological factors.
